title: Miten haluatte
summaries: "Miten haluatte" by William Shakespeare is a pastoral comedy written in 1599. When Rosalind flees her uncle's court to escape persecution, she seeks refuge in the Forest of Arden with her cousin Celia. There, amid a cast of memorable characters including the melancholy Jaques, she encounters love, disguise, and transformation. The forest becomes a stage where courtly exiles and country dwellers alike explore themes of romance, identity, and the contrast between city and rural life in one of Shakespeare's most beloved comedies.
